description_long: This object is a double-edged razor blade, commonly used in safety razors for shaving. It is rectangular with two long, straight, sharp edges on opposite sides, and features a series of cutouts and notches in the center for secure mounting in a razor handle. The blade is made of stainless steel, as indicated by the etched text 'STAINLESS STILL' on its surface. The central cutout is oval with two circular holes and two notched ends, allowing for precise alignment and stability. The blade is extremely thin, with a flat profile and a dark metallic finish. Its compact size and sharpness make it suitable for fine, controlled cutting tasks.
description: A thin, double-edged stainless steel razor blade with central cutouts and etched text, designed for precise shaving or cutting. The blade is flat, rectangular, and has a dark metallic finish.
description_view_0: The flat face of the razor blade is visible, showing the double-edged design, central cutouts, and etched 'STAINLESS STILL' text.
description_view_1: The blade is seen from the edge, appearing as a thin, straight line with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: The opposite flat face of the razor blade is visible, mirroring view 0 with the same cutouts and text.
description_view_3: The blade is again seen from the edge, identical to view 1, emphasizing its extreme thinness.
attribute_handle_design: no handle present; central cutouts for mounting
